Hell..personality: A profoundly passive, withdrawn individual who appears semi-idiotic or dazed, sitting in silence and staring blankly into space. They are indifferent to external impressions — unmoved by heat, cold, touch, or social interaction — and seem unable to project ideas or exert willpower over their own body. Fixed ideas take root and cannot be dislodged by reasoning or argument.
Hell..mental_state: The mind is benumbed and stupefied, with severely diminished concentration, memory, and the power of thought; the patient cannot rivet attention, cannot think clearly, and answers questions only after long delay or not at all. There is a characteristic loss of the mind's control over the body — muscles will not obey the will, creating a paralytic sluggishness rather than active delirium. When roused with effort, the patient stares with a dazed expression and may speak of seeing spirits or devils, but these hallucinations are shaped by prior learning rather than original ideation.
Hell..emotional_pattern: The core emotional state is deep apathy masquerading as melancholy — the patient appears sad and woeful but is largely incapable of active feeling or lamentation. Consolation aggravates the condition, yet there is no fear of death as in Aconite, nor the restless anguish of Aurum; instead there is a quiet, stubborn conviction of having sinned or committed an unpardonable wrong. A quasi-hysterical religious guilt may dominate, with fixed ideas about having sinned away one's day of grace, held with calm certainty rather than emotional torment.
